frame_label: Dress Code: Traditional = Respectable, Other = Questionable
generated_comment: There is nothing wrong if institutions expect a certain level of decency and discipline in dress. Our girls look far more graceful and respectable in Indian wear, and frankly when people dress in a very revealing way, unnecessary attention is bound to follow. This is not backward thinking, it is basic maryada and respect for the educational environment.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: The comment links women's clothing to respectability and subtly blames harassment on 'revealing' dress by saying attention is bound to follow. It encodes the frame through decency/maryada language, cultural superiority of Indian wear, and a clothing-harassment link.
original_frame: Dress Code: Traditional Equal Respectable, Other Equal Questionable.
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 45. Married. A Brahmin man in Pune. You are commenting under your real name on a news article shared on Facebook about a college in Maharashtra that introduced a dress code banning jeans and sleeveless tops for female students. Most commenters are criticising the college. You are writing a comment defending the dress code and explaining why you think institutions have the right and responsibility to regulate how young women dress and what happens in societies where there are no such standards.
